frameworks.name: speed-accuracy trade-off (computational modeling of latent decisional processes)
frameworks.one_line: Latent decisional processes driving strategic adjustment of speed vs accuracy

frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: Cathodal tDCS over left prefrontal and superior medial frontal cortex

frameworks.name: dopaminergic modulation of cognition
frameworks.one_line: Pharmacological dopamine manipulation interacts with stimulation effects

core_question: Does dopamine causally modulate how tDCS alters speed-accuracy trade-off decision-making?
key_assumptions: tDCS effects on cognition are at least partly dopamine-dependent
Computational modeling can dissociate latent decisional sub-processes
Dot-motion judgments engage ubiquitous strategic operations of the speed-accuracy trade-off
manipulations: Cathodal tDCS over left prefrontal and superior medial frontal cortex vs sham
Instructional emphasis: speed vs accuracy vs both
key_findings: Dopamine combined with tDCS impaired decision accuracy
Dopamine combined with tDCS impaired discriminability
Neither tDCS alone nor dopamine alone produced these impairments
interpretations: Dopamine-tDCS interaction altered the encoding or representation of discriminative evidence
Provides causal evidence for a dopaminergic role in tDCS effects on cognition and behavior
